\"components/xpath\" }\n   228→\n   229→# RSA key generation could be very slow without compilation\n   230→# optimizations, in development mode. Without optimizations, WPT might\n   231→# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n   232→# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n   233→# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n   234→# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n   235→[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\n   236→opt-level = 3\n   237→\n   238→# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n   239→# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n   240→# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n   241→[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\n   242→opt-level = 1\n   243→\n   244→[profile.release]\n   245→opt-level = 3\n   246→debug-assertions = true\n   247→\n   248→# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n   249→# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n   250→[profile.coverage]\n   251→inherits = \"dev\"\n   252→incremental = false\n   253→debug = false\n   254→\n   255→# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n   256→# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n   257→[profile.medium]\n   258→inherits = \"release\"\n   259→opt-level = 2\n   260→incremental = true\n   261→debug = \"line-tables-only\"\n   262→\n   263→[profile.production]\n   264→inherits = \"release\"\n   265→debug-assertions = false\n   266→lto = true\n   267→codegen-units = 1\n   268→opt-level = \"s\"\n   269→\n   270→[profile.production-stripped]\n   271→inherits = \"production\"\n   272→strip = true\n   273→\n   274→[profile.profiling]\n   275→inherits = \"release\"\n   276→debug-assertions = false\n   277→debug = true\n   278→lto = \"thin\"\n   279→codegen-units = 1\n   280→\n   281→[patch.crates-io]\n   282→# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n   283→# crate, add that here. Use the form:\n   284→#\n   285→#     <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n   286→#\n   287→# For html5ever:\n   288→#\n   289→# html5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/html5ever\" }\n   290→# markup5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/markup5ever\" }\n   291→# web_atoms = { path = \"../html5ever/web_atoms\" }\n   292→# xml5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/xml5ever\" }\n   293→# tendril = { path = \"../html5ever/tendril\" }\n   294→#\n   295→# Or for Stylo:\n   296→#\n   297→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\n   298→# selectors = { path = \"../stylo/selectors\" }\n   299→# servo_arc = { path = \"../stylo/servo_arc\" }\n   300→# stylo = { path = \"../stylo/style\" }\n   301→# stylo_atoms = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\n   302→# stylo_config = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_config\" }\n   303→# stylo_dom = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_dom\" }\n   304→# stylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\n   305→# stylo_traits = { path = \"../stylo/style_traits\" }\n   306→#\n   307→# Or for WebRender:\n   308→#\n   309→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/webrender\"]\n   310→# webrender = { path = \"../webrender/webrender\" }\n   311→# webrender_api = { path = \"../webrender/webrender_api\" }\n   312→# wr_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../webrender/wr_malloc_size_of\" }\n   313→#\n   314→# Or for another Git dependency:\n   315→#\n   316→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/<repository>\"]\n   317→# <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n   318→#\n   319→# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/rust-content-security-policy\"]\n   320→# content-security-policy = { path = \"../rust-content-security-policy\" }\n   321→#\n   322→# Or for mozjs:\n   323→#\n   324→# mozjs = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs\" }\n   325→# mozjs_sys = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs-sys\" }\n   326→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code DeferFontRelativeCustomPropertyResolution,\n    Name, SpecifiedValue,\n};\nuse style::dom::DummyAttributeProvider;\nuse style::font_metrics::FontMetrics;\nuse style::media_queries::{Device, style::values::computed::font::GenericFontFamily;\nuse style::values::computed::{Context, Length};\nuse style::values::specified::font::QueryFontMetricsFlags;\nuse test::{self, Bencher};\nuse url::Url;\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct DummyMetricsProvider;\n\nimpl FontMetricsProvider for DummyMetricsProvider {\n    fn query_font_metrics(\n        &self,\n        _vertical: bool,\n        _font: &Font,\n        _base_size: Length,\n        _flags: QueryFontMetricsFlags,\n    ) -> FontMetrics {\n        Default::default()\n    }\n    fn base_size_for_generic(&self, _: GenericFontFamily) -> Length {\n        Length::new(16.)\n    }\n}\n\nfn cascade(\n    name_and_value: &[(&str, &str)],\n    inherited: &ComputedCustomProperties,\n) -> ComputedCustomProperties {\n    let dummy_url_data = UrlExtraData::from(Url::parse(\"about:blank\").unwrap());\n    let declarations = name_and_value\n        .iter()\n        .map(|&(name, value)| {\n            let mut input = 600.),\n        Scale::new(1.0),\n        Box::new(DummyMetricsProvider),\n        initial_style,\n        PrefersColorScheme::Light,\n    );\n    let stylist = Stylist::new(device, QuirksMode::NoQuirks);\n    let mut builder = StyleBuilder::new(stylist.device(), Some(&stylist), None, None, None, false);\n    builder.custom_properties = inherited.clone();\n    let mut rule_cache_conditions = RuleCacheConditions::default();\n    let mut context = Context::new(\n        builder,\n        stylist.quirks_mode(),\n        &mut rule_cache_conditions,\n        ContainerSizeQuery::none(),\n    );\n    let mut builder = CustomPropertiesBuilder::new(&stylist, &mut context);\n\n    for declaration in &declarations {\n        builder.cascade(\n            declaration,\n            CascadePriority::new(CascadeLevel::same_tree_author_normal(), LayerOrder::root()),\n            &DummyAttributeProvider,\n        );\n    }\n\n    builder.build(\n        DeferFontRelativeCustomPropertyResolution::No,\n        &DummyAttributeProvider,\n    );\n    context.builder.custom_properties\n}\n\n#[bench]\nfn cascade_custom_simple(b: &mut Bencher) {\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GoTvtRPbvQqCcyNJuN2YaX","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→use std::cell::RefCell;\n     6→\n     7→use cssparser::SourceLocation;\n     8→use servo_arc::Arc;\n     9→use style::context::QuirksMode;\n    10→use style::error_reporting::{ContextualParseError, ParseErrorReporter};\n    11→use style::media_queries::MediaList;\n    12→use style::shared_lock::SharedRwLock;\n    13→use style::stylesheets::{\n    14→    AllowImportRules, Origin, Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument, UrlExtraData,\n    15→};\n    16→use url::Url;\n    17→\n    18→#[derive(Debug)]\n    19→struct CSSError {\n    20→    pub url: Arc<Url>,\n    21→    pub line: u32,\n    22→    pub column: u32,\n    23→    pub message: String,\n    24→}\n    25→\n    26→struct TestingErrorReporter {\n    27→    errors: RefCell<Vec<CSSError>>,\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→impl TestingErrorReporter {\n    31→    pub fn new() -> Self {\n    32→        TestingErrorReporter {\n    33→            errors: RefCell::new(Vec::new()),\n    34→        }\n    35→    }\n    36→\n    37→    fn assert_messages_contain(&self, expected_errors: &[(u32, u32, &str)]) {\n    38→        let errors = self.errors.borrow();\n    39→        for (i, (error, &(line, column, message))) in errors.iter().zip(expected_errors).enumerate()\n    40→        {\n    41→            assert_eq!(\n    42→                (error.line, error.column),\n    43→                (line, column),\n    44→                \"line/column numbers of the {}th error: {:?}\",\n    45→                i + 1,\n    46→                error.message\n    47→            );\n    48→            assert!(\n    49→                error.message.contains(message),\n    50→                \"{:?} does not contain {:?}\",\n    51→                error.message,\n    52→                message\n    53→            );\n    54→        }\n    55→        if errors.len() < expected_errors.len() {\n    56→            panic!(\"Missing errors: {:#?}\", &expected_errors[errors.len()..]);\n    57→        }\n    58→        if errors.len() > expected_errors.len() {\n    59→            panic!(\"Extra errors: {:#?}\", &errors[expected_errors.len()..]);\n    60→        }\n    61→    }\n    62→}\n    63→\n    64→impl ParseErrorReporter for TestingErrorReporter {\n    65→    fn report_error(\n    66→        &self,\n    67→        url: &UrlExtraData,\n    68→        location: SourceLocation,\n    69→        error: ContextualParseError,\n    70→    ) {\n    71→        self.errors.borrow_mut().push(CSSError {\n    72→            url: url.0.clone(),\n    73→            line: location.line,\n    74→            column: location.column,\n    75→            message: error.to_string(),\n    76→        })\n    77→    }\n    78→}\n    79→\n    80→#[test]\n    81→fn test_report_error_stylesheet() {\n    82→    let css = r\"\n    83→    div {\n    84→        background-color: red;\n    85→        display: invalid;\n    86→        background-image: linear-gradient(0deg, black, invalid, transparent);\n    87→        invalid: true;\n    88→    }\n    89→    @media (min-width: 10px invalid 1000px) {}\n    90→    @font-face { src: url(), invalid, url(); }\n    91→    @counter-style foo { symbols: a 0invalid b }\n    92→    @font-feature-values Sans Sans { @foo {} @swash { foo: 1 invalid 2 } }\n    93→    @invalid;\n    94→    @media screen { @invalid; }\n    95→    @supports (color: green) and invalid and (margin: 0) {}\n    96→    @keyframes foo { from invalid {} to { margin: 0 invalid 0; } }\n    97→    \";\n    98→    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    99→    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n   100→\n   101→    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n   102→    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n   103→    Stylesheet::from_str(\n   104→        css,\n   105→        url.clone().into(),\n   106→        Origin::UserAgent,\n   107→        media,\n   108→        lock,\n   109→        None,\n   110→        Some(&error_reporter),\n   111→ 185→            Some(\"something\".to_string()),\n   186→        ),\n   187→    ];\n   188→\n   189→    for test in tests {\n   190→        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n   191→ consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c9b824cf-efe9-40d1-9d4c-1371550429b3","uuid":"fa64060d-5a24-4709-b1d5-8b6a041d2440","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/tests/unit/style/stylesheets.rs","content":"/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\nuse std::cell::RefCell;\n\nuse cssparser::SourceLocation;\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse style::context::QuirksMode;\nuse style::error_reporting::{ContextualParseError, ParseErrorReporter};\nuse style::media_queries::MediaList;\nuse style::shared_lock::SharedRwLock;\nuse style::stylesheets::{\n    AllowImportRules, Origin, Stylesheet, StylesheetInDocument, UrlExtraData,\n};\nuse url::Url;\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct CSSError {\n    pub url: Arc<Url>,\n    pub line: u32,\n    pub column: u32,\n    pub message: String,\n}\n\nstruct TestingErrorReporter {\n    errors: RefCell<Vec<CSSError>>,\n}\n\nimpl TestingErrorReporter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        TestingErrorReporter {\n            errors: RefCell::new(Vec::new()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn assert_messages_contain(&self, expected_errors: &[(u32, u32, &str)]) {\n        let errors = self.errors.borrow();\n        for (i, (error, &(line, column, message))) in errors.iter().zip(expected_errors).enumerate()\n        {\n            assert_eq!(\n                (error.line, error.column),\n                (line, column),\n                \"line/column numbers of the {}th error: {:?}\",\n                i + 1,\n                error.message\n            );\n            assert!(\n                error.message.contains(message),\n                \"{:?} does not contain {:?}\",\n                error.message,\n                message\n            );\n        }\n        if errors.len() < expected_errors.len() {\n            panic!(\"Missing errors: {:#?}\", &expected_errors[errors.len()..]);\n        }\n        if errors.len() > expected_errors.len() {\n            panic!(\"Extra errors: {:#?}\", &errors[expected_errors.len()..]);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ParseErrorReporter for TestingErrorReporter {\n    fn report_error(\n        &self,\n        url: &UrlExtraData,\n        location: SourceLocation,\n        error: ContextualParseError,\n    ) {\n        self.errors.borrow_mut().push(CSSError {\n            url: url.0.clone(),\n            line: location.line,\n            column: location.column,\n            message: error.to_string(),\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_report_error_stylesheet() {\n    let css = r\"\n    div {\n        background-color: red;\n        display: invalid;\n        background-image: linear-gradient(0deg, black, invalid, transparent);\n        invalid: true;\n    }\n    @media (min-width: 10px invalid 1000px) {}\n    @font-face { src: url(), invalid, url(); }\n    @counter-style foo { symbols: a 0invalid b }\n    @font-feature-values Sans Sans { @foo {} @swash { foo: 1 invalid 2 } }\n    @invalid;\n    @media screen { @invalid; }\n    @supports (color: green) and invalid and (margin: 0) {}\n    @keyframes foo { from invalid {} to { margin: 0 invalid 0; } }\n    \";\n    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n\n    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n    Stylesheet::from_str(\n        css,\n        url.clone().into(),\n        Origin::UserAgent,\n        media,\n        lock,\n        None,\n        Some(&error_reporter),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n    );\n\n    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[\n        (\n            3,\n            18,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'display: invalid;'\",\n        ),\n        (\n            4,\n            43,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'background-image:\",\n        ), // FIXME: column should be around 56\n        (5, 17, \"Unsupported property declaration: 'invalid: true;'\"),\n        (7, 28, \"Invalid media rule\"),\n        // When @counter-style is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n        (9, 19, \"Invalid rule: '@counter-style \"),\n        // When @font-feature-values is supported, this should be replaced with two errors\n        (10, 25, \"Invalid rule: '@font-feature-values \"),\n        (11, 13, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n        (12, 29, \"Invalid rule: '@invalid'\"),\n        (13, 34, \"Invalid rule: '@supports \"),\n        (14, 26, \"Invalid keyframe rule: 'from invalid '\"),\n        (\n            14,\n            52,\n            \"Unsupported property declaration: 'margin: 0 invalid 0;'\",\n        ),\n    ]);\n\n    assert_eq!(*error_reporter.errors.borrow()[0].url, url);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_report_unrecognized_vendor_properties() {\n    let css = r\"\n    div {\n        -o-background-color: red;\n        _background-color: red;\n        -moz-background-color: red;\n    }\n    \";\n    let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n    let error_reporter = TestingErrorReporter::new();\n\n    let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n    let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n    Stylesheet::from_str(\n        css,\n        url.into(),\n        Origin::UserAgent,\n        media,\n        lock,\n        None,\n        Some(&error_reporter),\n        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n        AllowImportRules::Yes,\n    );\n\n    error_reporter.assert_messages_contain(&[(\n        4,\n        31,\n        \"Unsupported property declaration: '-moz-background-color: red;'\",\n    )]);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_source_map_url() {\n    let tests = vec![\n        (\"\", None),\n        (\n            \"/*# sourceMappingURL=something */\",\n            Some(\"something\".to_string()),\n        ),\n    ];\n\n    for test in tests {\n        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n        let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n        let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n        let stylesheet = Stylesheet::from_str(\n            test.0,\n            url.into(),\n            Origin::UserAgent,\n            media,\n            lock.clone(),\n            None,\n            None,\n            QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n            AllowImportRules::Yes,\n        );\n        let guard = lock.read();\n        let url_opt = stylesheet.contents(&guard).source_map_url.clone();\n        assert_eq!(url_opt, test.1);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_source_url() {\n    let tests = vec![\n        (\"\", None),\n        (\"/*# sourceURL=something */\", Some(\"something\".to_string())),\n    ];\n\n    for test in tests {\n        let url = Url::parse(\"about::test\").unwrap();\n        let lock = SharedRwLock::new();\n        let media = Arc::new(lock.wrap(MediaList::empty()));\n        let stylesheet = Stylesheet::from_str(\n            test.0,\n            url.into(),\n            Origin::UserAgent,\n            media,\n            lock.clone(),\n            None,\n            None,\n            QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n            AllowImportRules::Yes,\n        );\n        let guard = lock.read();\n        let url_opt = stylesheet.contents(&guard).source_url.clone();\n        assert_eq!(url_opt, test.1);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":237,"startLine":1,"totalLines":237}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019BunmhZhqdkacQgUrSR9FS","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→use app_units::{Au, MAX_AU};\n     6→use base::id::{BrowsingContextId, PipelineId};\n     7→use data_url::DataUrl;\n     8→use embedder_traits::ViewportDetails;\n     9→use euclid::{Scale, Size2D};\n    10→use html5ever::local_name;\n    11→use layout_api::wrapper_traits::ThreadSafeLayoutNode;\n    12→use layout_api::{IFrameSize, LayoutImageDestination, SVGElementData};\n    13→use malloc_size_of_derive::MallocSizeOf;\n    14→use net_traits::image_cache::{Image, ImageOrMetadataAvailable, VectorImage};\n    15→use script::layout_dom::ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode;\n    16→use selectors::Element;\n    17→use servo_arc::Arc as ServoArc;\n    18→use style::Zero;\n    19→use style::attr::AttrValue;\n    20→use style::computed_values::object_fit::T as ObjectFit;\n    21→use style::logical_geometry::{Direction, WritingMode};\n    22→use style::properties::{ComputedValues, StyleBuilder};\n    23→use style::rule_cache::RuleCacheConditions;\n    24→use style::servo::url::ComputedUrl;\n    25→use style::stylesheets::container_rule::ContainerSizeQuery;\n    26→use style::values::CSSFloat;\n    27→use style::values::computed::image::Image as ComputedImage;\n    28→use style::values::computed::{Content, Context, ToComputedValue};\n    29→use style::values::generics::counters::{GenericContentItem, GenericContentItems};\n    30→use url::Url;\n    31→use webrender_api::ImageKey;\n    32→\n    33→use crate::cell::ArcRefCell;\n    34→use crate::context::{LayoutContext, LayoutImageCacheResult};\n    35→use crate::dom::NodeExt;\n    36→use crate::fragment_tree::{\n    37→    BaseFragment, BaseFragmentInfo, CollapsedBlockMargins, Fragment, IFrameFragment, ImageFragment,\n    38→};\n    39→use crate::geom::{LogicalVec2, PhysicalPoint, PhysicalRect, PhysicalSize};\n    40→use crate::layout_box_base::{CacheableLayoutResult, LayoutBoxBase};\n    41→use crate::sizing::{\n    42→    ComputeInlineContentSizes, InlineContentSizesResult, LazySize, SizeConstraint,\n    43→};\n    44→use crate::style_ext::{AspectRatio, Clamp, ComputedValuesExt, LayoutStyle};\n    45→use crate::{ConstraintSpace, ContainingBlock};\n    46→\n    47→#[derive(Debug, MallocSizeOf)]\n    48→pub(crate) struct ReplacedContents {\n    49→    pub kind: ReplacedContentKind,\n    50→    natural_size: NaturalSizes,\n    51→    base_fragment_info: BaseFragmentInfo,\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/// The natural dimensions of a replaced element, including a height, width, and\n    55→/// aspect ratio.\n    56→///\n    57→/// * Raster images always have an natural width and height, with 1 image pixel = 1px.\n    58→///   The natural ratio should be based on dividing those.\n    59→///   See <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/4572> for the case where either is zero.\n    60→///   PNG specifically disallows this but I (SimonSapin) am not sure about other formats.\n    61→///\n    62→/// * Form controls have both natural width and height **but no natural ratio**.\n    63→///   See <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/1044> and\n    64→///   <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images/#natural-dimensions> “In general, […]”\n    65→///\n    66→/// * For SVG, see <https://svgwg.org/svg2-draft/coords.html#SizingSVGInCSS>\n    67→///   and again <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/4572>.\n    68→///\n    69→/// * IFrames do not have natural width and height or natural ratio according\n    70→///   to <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images/#intrinsic-dimensions>.\n    71→#[derive(Debug, MallocSizeOf)]\n    72→pub(crate) struct NaturalSizes {\n    73→    pub width: Option<Au>,\n    74→    pub height: Option<Au>,\n    75→    pub ratio: Option<CSSFloat>,\n    76→}\n    77→\n    78→impl NaturalSizes {\n    79→    pub(crate) fn from_width_and_height(width: f32, height: f32) -> Self {\n    80→        // https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images/#natural-aspect-ratio:\n    81→        // \"If an object has a degenerate natural aspect ratio (at least one part being\n    82→        // zero or infinity), it is treated as having no natural aspect ratio.\"\"\n    83→        let ratio = if width.is_normal() && height.is_normal() {\n    84→            Some(width / height)\n    85→        } else {\n    86→            None\n    87→        };\n    88→\n    89→        Self {\n    90→            width: Some(Au::from_f32_px(width)),\n    91→            height: Some(Au::from_f32_px(height)),\n    92→            ratio,\n    93→        }\n    94→    }\n    95→\n    96→    pub(crate) fn from_natural_size_in_dots(natural_size_in_dots: PhysicalSize<f64>) -> Self {\n    97→        // FIXME: should 'image-resolution' (when implemented) be used *instead* of\n    98→        // `script::dom::htmlimageelement::ImageRequest::current_pixel_density`?\n    99→        // https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images-4/#the-image-resolution\n   100→        let dppx = 1.0;\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. crate::cell::ArcRefCell;\nuse crate::context::{LayoutContext, LayoutImageCacheResult};\nuse crate::dom::NodeExt;\nuse crate::fragment_tree::{\n    BaseFragment, BaseFragmentInfo, CollapsedBlockMargins, Fragment, IFrameFragment, ImageFragment,\n};\nuse crate::geom::{LogicalVec2, PhysicalPoint, PhysicalRect, PhysicalSize};\nuse crate::layout_box_base::{CacheableLayoutResult, LayoutBoxBase};\nuse crate::sizing::{\n    ComputeInlineContentSizes, InlineContentSizesResult, LazySize, SizeConstraint,\n};\nuse crate::style_ext::{AspectRatio, Clamp, ComputedValuesExt, LayoutStyle};\nuse crate::{ConstraintSpace, ContainingBlock};\n\n#[derive(Debug, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct ReplacedContents {\n    pub kind: ReplacedContentKind,\n    natural_size: NaturalSizes,\n    base_fragment_info: BaseFragmentInfo,\n}\n\n/// The natural dimensions of a replaced element, including a height, width, and\n/// aspect ratio.\n///\n/// * Raster images always have an natural width and height, with 1 image pixel = 1px.\n///   The natural ratio should be based on dividing those.\n///   See <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/4572> for the case where either is zero.\n///   PNG specifically disallows this but I (SimonSapin) am not sure about other formats.\n///\n/// * Form controls have both natural width and height **but no natural ratio**.\n///   See <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/1044> and\n///   <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images/#natural-dimensions> “In general, […]”\n///\n/// * For SVG, see <https://svgwg.org/svg2-draft/coords.html#SizingSVGInCSS>\n///   and again <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/4572>.\n///\n/// * IFrames do not have natural width and height or natural ratio according\n///   to <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images/#intrinsic-dimensions>.\n#[derive(Debug, MallocSizeOf)]\npub(crate) struct NaturalSizes {\n    pub width: Option<Au>,\n    pub height: Option<Au>,\n    pub ratio: Option<CSSFloat>,\n}\n\nimpl NaturalSizes {\n    pub(crate) fn from_width_and_height(width: f32, height: f32) -> Self {\n        // https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images/#natural-aspect-ratio:\n        // \"If an object has a degenerate natural aspect ratio (at least one part being\n        // zero or infinity), it is treated as having no natural aspect ratio.\"\"\n        let ratio = if width.is_normal() && height.is_normal() {\n            Some(width / height)\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        Self {\n            width: Some(Au::from_f32_px(width)),\n            height: Some(Au::from_f32_px(height)),\n            ratio,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn from_natural_size_in_dots(natural_size_in_dots: PhysicalSize<f64>) -> Self {\n        // FIXME: should 'image-resolution' (when implemented) be used *instead* of\n        // `script::dom::htmlimageelement::ImageRequest::current_pixel_density`?\n        // https://drafts.csswg.org/css-images-4/#the-image-resolution\n        let dppx = 1.0;","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":731}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016FN2wBKDvjfXT85MnhvRzw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→//! A [`@container`][container] rule.\n     6→//!\n     7→//! [container]: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-rule\n     8→\n     9→use crate::computed_value_flags::ComputedValueFlags;\n    10→use crate::derives::*;\n    11→use crate::dom::TElement;\n    12→use crate::logical_geometry::{LogicalSize, WritingMode};\n    13→use crate::parser::ParserContext;\n    14→use crate::properties::ComputedValues;\n    15→use crate::queries::feature::{AllowsRanges, Evaluator, FeatureFlags, QueryFeatureDescription};\n    16→use crate::queries::values::Orientation;\n    17→use crate::queries::{FeatureType, QueryCondition};\n    18→use crate::shared_lock::{\n    19→    DeepCloneWithLock, Locked, SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard,\n    20→};\n    21→use crate::stylesheets::{CssRules, CustomMediaEvaluator};\n    22→use crate::stylist::Stylist;\n    23→use crate::values::computed::{CSSPixelLength, ContainerType, Context, Ratio};\n    24→use crate::values::specified::ContainerName;\n    25→use app_units::Au;\n    26→use cssparser::{Parser, SourceLocation};\n    27→use euclid::default::Size2D;\n    28→#[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\n    29→use malloc_size_of::{MallocSizeOfOps, MallocUnconditionalShallowSizeOf};\n    30→use selectors::kleene_value::KleeneValue;\n    31→use servo_arc::Arc;\n    32→use std::fmt::{self, Write};\n    33→use style_traits::{CssStringWriter, CssWriter, ParseError, ToCss};\n    34→\n    35→/// A container rule.\n    36→#[derive(Debug, ToShmem)]\n    37→pub struct ContainerRule {\n    38→    /// The container query and name.\n    39→    pub condition: Arc<ContainerCondition>,\n    40→    /// The nested rules inside the block.\n    41→    pub rules: Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\n    42→    /// The source position where this rule was found.\n    43→    pub source_location: SourceLocation,\n    44→}\n    45→\n    46→impl ContainerRule {\n    47→    /// Returns the query condition.\n    48→    pub fn query_condition(&self) -> &QueryCondition {\n    49→        &self.condition.condition\n    50→    }\n    51→\n    52→    /// Returns the query name filter.\n    53→    pub fn container_name(&self) -> &ContainerName {\n    54→        &self.condition.name\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    /// Measure heap usage.\n    58→    #[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\n    59→    pub fn size_of(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, ops: &mut MallocSizeOfOps) -> usize {\n    60→        // Measurement of other fields may be added later.\n    61→        self.rules.unconditional_shallow_size_of(ops)\n    62→            + self.rules.read_with(guard).size_of(guard, ops)\n    63→    }\n    64→}\n    65→\n    66→impl DeepCloneWithLock for ContainerRule {\n    67→    fn deep_clone_with_lock(&self, lock: &SharedRwLock, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) -> Self {\n    68→        let rules = self.rules.read_with(guard);\n    69→        Self {\n    70→            condition: self.condition.clone(),\n    71→            rules: Arc::new(lock.wrap(rules.deep_clone_with_lock(lock, guard))),\n    72→            source_location: self.source_location.clone(),\n    73→        }\n    74→    }\n    75→}\n    76→\n    77→impl ToCssWithGuard for ContainerRule {\n    78→    fn to_css(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, dest: &mut CssStringWriter) -> fmt::Result {\n    79→        dest.write_str(\"@container \")?;\n    80→        {\n    81→            let mut writer = CssWriter::new(dest);\n    82→            if !self.condition.name.is_none() {\n    83→                self.condition.name.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n    84→                writer.write_char(' ')?;\n    85→            }\n    86→            self.condition.condition.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n    87→        }\n    88→        self.rules.read_with(guard).to_css_block(guard, dest)\n    89→    }\n    90→}\n    91→\n    92→/// A container condition and filter, combined.\n    93→#[derive(Debug, ToShmem, ToCss)]\n    94→pub struct ContainerCondition {\n    95→    #[css(skip_if = \"ContainerName::is_none\")]\n    96→    name: ContainerName,\n    97→    condition: QueryCondition,\n    98→    #[css(skip)]\n    99→    flags: FeatureFlags,\n   100→}\n   101→\n   102→/// The result of a successful container query lookup.\n   103→pub struct ContainerLookupResult<E> {\n   104→    /// The relevant container.\n   105→    pub element: E,\n   106→    /// The sizing / writing-mode information of the container.\n   107→    pub info: ContainerInfo,\n   108→    /// The style of the element.\n   109→    pub style: Arc<ComputedValues>,\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→fn container_type_axes(ty_: ContainerType, wm: WritingMode) -> FeatureFlags {\n   113→    if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n   114→        FeatureFlags::all_container_axes()\n   115→    } else if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n   116→        let physical_axis = if wm.is_vertical() {\n   117→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS\n   118→        } else {\n   119→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS\n   120→        };\n   121→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS | physical_axis\n   122→    } else {\n   123→        FeatureFlags::empty()\n   124→    }\n   125→}\n   126→\n   127→enum TraversalResult<T> {\n   128→    InProgress,\n   129→    StopTraversal,\n   130→    Done(T),\n   131→}\n   132→\n   133→fn traverse_container<E, F, R>(\n   134→    mut e: E,\n   135→    originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   136→    evaluator: F,\n   137→) -> Option<(E, R)>\n   138→where\n   139→    E: TElement,\n   140→    F: Fn(E, Option<&ComputedValues>) -> TraversalResult<R>,\n   141→{\n   142→    if originating_element_style.is_some() {\n   143→        match evaluator(e, originating_element_style) {\n   144→            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n   145→            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n   146→            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((e, result)),\n   147→        }\n   148→    }\n   149→    while let Some(element) = e.traversal_parent() {\n   150→        match evaluator(element, None) {\n   151→            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n   152→            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n   153→            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((element, result)),\n   154→        }\n   155→        e = element;\n   156→    }\n   157→\n   158→    None\n   159→}\n   160→\n   161→impl ContainerCondition {\n   162→    /// Parse a container condition.\n   163→    pub fn parse<'a>(\n   164→        context: &ParserContext,\n   165→        input: &mut Parser<'a, '_>,\n   166→    ) -> Result<Self, ParseError<'a>> {\n   167→        let name = input\n   168→            .try_parse(|input| ContainerName::parse_for_query(context, input))\n   169→            .ok()\n   170→            .unwrap_or_else(ContainerName::none);\n   171→        let condition = QueryCondition::parse(context, input, FeatureType::Container)?;\n   172→        let flags = condition.cumulative_flags();\n   173→        Ok(Self {\n   174→            name,\n   175→            condition,\n   176→            flags,\n   177→        })\n   178→    }\n   179→\n   180→    fn valid_container_info<E>(\n   181→        &self,\n   182→        potential_container: E,\n   183→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   184→    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n   185→    where\n   186→        E: TElement,\n   187→    {\n   188→        let data;\n   189→        let style = match originating_element_style {\n   190→            Some(s) => s,\n   191→            None => {\n   192→                data = match potential_container.borrow_data() {\n   193→                    Some(d) => d,\n   194→                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n   195→                };\n   196→                &**data.styles.primary()\n   197→            },\n   198→        };\n   199→        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n   200→        let box_style = style.get_box();\n   201→\n   202→        // Filter by container-type.\n   203→        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n   204→        let available_axes = container_type_axes(container_type, wm);\n   205→        if !available_axes.contains(self.flags.container_axes()) {\n   206→            return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n   207→        }\n   208→\n   209→        // Filter by container-name.\n   210→        let container_name = box_style.clone_container_name();\n   211→        for filter_name in self.name.0.iter() {\n   212→            if !container_name.0.contains(filter_name) {\n   213→                return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n   214→            }\n   215→        }\n   216→\n   217→        let size = potential_container.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n   218→        let style = style.to_arc();\n   219→        TraversalResult::Done(ContainerLookupResult {\n   220→            element: potential_container,\n   221→            info: ContainerInfo { size, wm },\n   222→            style,\n   223→        })\n   224→    }\n   225→\n   226→    /// Performs container lookup for a given element.\n   227→    pub fn find_container<E>(\n   228→        &self,\n   229→        e: E,\n   230→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   231→    ) -> Option<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n   232→    where\n   233→        E: TElement,\n   234→    {\n   235→        match traverse_container(\n   236→            e,\n   237→            originating_element_style,\n   238→            |element, originating_element_style| {\n   239→                self.valid_container_info(element, originating_element_style)\n   240→            },\n   241→        ) {\n   242→            Some((_, result)) => Some(result),\n   243→            None => None,\n   244→        }\n   245→    }\n   246→\n   247→    /// Tries to match a container query condition for a given element.\n   248→    pub(crate) fn matches<E>(\n   249→        &self,\n   250→        stylist: &Stylist,\n   251→        element: E,\n   252→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   253→        invalidation_flags: &mut ComputedValueFlags,\n   254→    ) -> KleeneValue\n   255→    where\n   256→        E: TElement,\n   257→    {\n   258→        let result = self.find_container(element, originating_element_style);\n   259→        let (container, info) = match result {\n   260→            Some(r) => (Some(r.element), Some((r.info, r.style))),\n   261→            None => (None, None),\n   262→        };\n   263→        // Set up the lookup for the container in question, as the condition may be using container\n   264→        // query lengths.\n   265→        let size_query_container_lookup = ContainerSizeQuery::for_option_element(\n   266→            container, /* known_parent_style = */ None, /* is_pseudo = */ false,\n   267→        );\n   268→        Context::for_container_query_evaluation(\n   269→            stylist.device(),\n   270→            Some(stylist),\n   271→            info,\n   272→            size_query_container_lookup,\n   273→            |context| {\n   274→                let matches = self\n   275→                    .condition\n   276→                    .matches(context, &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none());\n   277→                if context\n   278→                    .style()\n   279→                    .flags()\n   280→                    .contains(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS)\n   281→                {\n   282→                    // TODO(emilio): Might need something similar to improve\n   283→                    // invalidation of font relative container-query lengths.\n   284→                    invalidation_flags\n   285→                        .insert(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS_ON_CONTAINER_QUERIES);\n   286→                }\n   287→                matches\n   288→            },\n   289→        )\n   290→    }\n   291→}\n   292→\n   293→/// Information needed to evaluate an individual container query.\n   294→#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\n   295→pub struct ContainerInfo {\n   296→    size: Size2D<Option<Au>>,\n   297→    wm: WritingMode,\n   298→}\n   299→\n   300→impl ContainerInfo {\n   301→    fn size(&self) -> Option<Size2D<Au>> {\n   302→        Some(Size2D::new(self.size.width?, self.size.height?))\n   303→    }\n   304→}\n   305→\n   306→fn eval_width(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   307→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   308→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.width?.to_f32_px()))\n   309→}\n   310→\n   311→fn eval_height(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   312→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   313→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.height?.to_f32_px()))\n   314→}\n   315→\n   316→fn eval_inline_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   317→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   318→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n   319→        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n   320→            .inline?\n   321→            .to_f32_px(),\n   322→    ))\n   323→}\n   324→\n   325→fn eval_block_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n   326→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   327→    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n   328→        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n   329→            .block?\n   330→            .to_f32_px(),\n   331→    ))\n   332→}\n   333→\n   334→fn eval_aspect_ratio(context: &Context) -> Option<Ratio> {\n   335→    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n   336→    Some(Ratio::new(\n   337→        info.size.width?.0 as f32,\n   338→        info.size.height?.0 as f32,\n   339→    ))\n   340→}\n   341→\n   342→fn eval_orientation(context: &Context, value: Option<Orientation>) -> KleeneValue {\n   343→    let size = match context.container_info.as_ref().and_then(|info| info.size()) {\n   344→        Some(size) => size,\n   345→        None => return KleeneValue::Unknown,\n   346→    };\n   347→    KleeneValue::from(Orientation::eval(size, value))\n   348→}\n   349→\n   350→/// https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-features\n   351→///\n   352→/// TODO: Support style queries, perhaps.\n   353→pub static CONTAINER_FEATURES: [QueryFeatureDescription; 6] = [\n   354→    feature!(\n   355→        atom!(\"width\"),\n   356→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   357→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_width),\n   358→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS,\n   359→    ),\n   360→    feature!(\n   361→        atom!(\"height\"),\n   362→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   363→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_height),\n   364→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS,\n   365→    ),\n   366→    feature!(\n   367→        atom!(\"inline-size\"),\n   368→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   369→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_inline_size),\n   370→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS,\n   371→    ),\n   372→    feature!(\n   373→        atom!(\"block-size\"),\n   374→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   375→        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_block_size),\n   376→        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS,\n   377→    ),\n   378→    feature!(\n   379→        atom!(\"aspect-ratio\"),\n   380→        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n   381→        Evaluator::OptionalNumberRatio(eval_aspect_ratio),\n   382→        // XXX from_bits_truncate is const, but the pipe operator isn't, so this\n   383→        // works around it.\n   384→        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n   385→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n   386→                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n   387→        ),\n   388→    ),\n   389→    feature!(\n   390→        atom!(\"orientation\"),\n   391→        AllowsRanges::No,\n   392→        keyword_evaluator!(eval_orientation, Orientation),\n   393→        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n   394→            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n   395→                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n   396→        ),\n   397→    ),\n   398→];\n   399→\n   400→/// Result of a container size query, signifying the hypothetical containment boundary in terms of physical axes.\n   401→/// Defined by up to two size containers. Queries on logical axes are resolved with respect to the querying\n   402→/// element's writing mode.\n   403→#[derive(Copy, Clone, Default)]\n   404→pub struct ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   405→    width: Option<Au>,\n   406→    height: Option<Au>,\n   407→}\n   408→\n   409→impl ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   410→    fn get_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> Size2D<Au> {\n   411→        use crate::values::specified::ViewportVariant;\n   412→        context.viewport_size_for_viewport_unit_resolution(ViewportVariant::Small)\n   413→    }\n   414→\n   415→    fn get_logical_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> LogicalSize<Au> {\n   416→        LogicalSize::from_physical(\n   417→            context.builder.writing_mode,\n   418→            Self::get_viewport_size(context),\n   419→        )\n   420→    }\n   421→\n   422→    /// Get the inline-size of the query container.\n   423→    pub fn get_container_inline_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   424→        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n   425→            if let Some(w) = self.width {\n   426→                return w;\n   427→            }\n   428→        } else {\n   429→            if let Some(h) = self.height {\n   430→                return h;\n   431→            }\n   432→        }\n   433→        Self::get_logical_viewport_size(context).inline\n   434→    }\n   435→\n   436→    /// Get the block-size of the query container.\n   437→    pub fn get_container_block_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   438→        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n   439→            self.get_container_height(context)\n   440→        } else {\n   441→            self.get_container_width(context)\n   442→        }\n   443→    }\n   444→\n   445→    /// Get the width of the query container.\n   446→    pub fn get_container_width(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   447→        if let Some(w) = self.width {\n   448→            return w;\n   449→        }\n   450→        Self::get_viewport_size(context).width\n   451→    }\n   452→\n   453→    /// Get the height of the query container.\n   454→    pub fn get_container_height(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n   455→        if let Some(h) = self.height {\n   456→            return h;\n   457→        }\n   458→        Self::get_viewport_size(context).height\n   459→    }\n   460→\n   461→    // Merge the result of a subsequent lookup, preferring the initial result.\n   462→    fn merge(self, new_result: Self) -> Self {\n   463→        let mut result = self;\n   464→        if let Some(width) = new_result.width {\n   465→            result.width.get_or_insert(width);\n   466→        }\n   467→        if let Some(height) = new_result.height {\n   468→            result.height.get_or_insert(height);\n   469→        }\n   470→        result\n   471→    }\n   472→\n   473→    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {\n   474→        self.width.is_some() && self.height.is_some()\n   475→    }\n   476→}\n   477→\n   478→/// Unevaluated lazy container size query.\n   479→pub enum ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n   480→    /// Query prior to evaluation.\n   481→    NotEvaluated(Box<dyn Fn() -> ContainerSizeQueryResult + 'a>),\n   482→    /// Cached evaluated result.\n   483→    Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult),\n   484→}\n   485→\n   486→impl<'a> ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n   487→    fn evaluate_potential_size_container<E>(\n   488→        e: E,\n   489→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   490→    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerSizeQueryResult>\n   491→    where\n   492→        E: TElement,\n   493→    {\n   494→        let data;\n   495→        let style = match originating_element_style {\n   496→            Some(s) => s,\n   497→            None => {\n   498→                data = match e.borrow_data() {\n   499→                    Some(d) => d,\n   500→                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n   501→                };\n   502→                &**data.styles.primary()\n   503→            },\n   504→        };\n   505→        if !style\n   506→            .flags\n   507→            .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n   508→        {\n   509→            // We know we won't find a size container.\n   510→            return TraversalResult::StopTraversal;\n   511→        }\n   512→\n   513→        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n   514→        let box_style = style.get_box();\n   515→\n   516→        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n   517→        let size = e.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n   518→        if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n   519→            TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   520→                width: size.width,\n   521→                height: size.height,\n   522→            })\n   523→        } else if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n   524→            if wm.is_horizontal() {\n   525→                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   526→                    width: size.width,\n   527→                    height: None,\n   528→                })\n   529→            } else {\n   530→                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   531→                    width: None,\n   532→                    height: size.height,\n   533→                })\n   534→            }\n   535→        } else {\n   536→            TraversalResult::InProgress\n   537→        }\n   538→    }\n   539→\n   540→    /// Find the query container size for a given element. Meant to be used as a callback for new().\n   541→    fn lookup<E>(\n   542→        element: E,\n   543→        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n   544→    ) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult\n   545→    where\n   546→        E: TElement + 'a,\n   547→    {\n   548→        match traverse_container(\n   549→            element,\n   550→            originating_element_style,\n   551→            |e, originating_element_style| {\n   552→                Self::evaluate_potential_size_container(e, originating_element_style)\n   553→            },\n   554→        ) {\n   555→            Some((container, result)) => {\n   556→                if result.is_complete() {\n   557→                    result\n   558→                } else {\n   559→                    // Traverse up from the found size container to see if we can get a complete containment.\n   560→                    result.merge(Self::lookup(container, None))\n   561→                }\n   562→            },\n   563→            None => ContainerSizeQueryResult::default(),\n   564→        }\n   565→    }\n   566→\n   567→    /// Create a new instance of the container size query for given element, with a deferred lookup callback.\n   568→    pub fn for_element<E>(\n   569→        element: E,\n   570→        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n   571→        is_pseudo: bool,\n   572→    ) -> Self\n   573→    where\n   574→        E: TElement + 'a,\n   575→    {\n   576→        let parent;\n   577→        let data;\n   578→        let parent_style = match known_parent_style {\n   579→            Some(s) => Some(s),\n   580→            None => {\n   581→                // No need to bother if we're the top element.\n   582→                parent = match element.traversal_parent() {\n   583→                    Some(parent) => parent,\n   584→                    None => return Self::none(),\n   585→                };\n   586→                data = parent.borrow_data();\n   587→                data.as_ref().map(|data| &**data.styles.primary())\n   588→            },\n   589→        };\n   590→\n   591→        // If there's no style, such as being `display: none` or so, we still want to show a\n   592→        // correct computed value, so give it a try.\n   593→        let should_traverse = parent_style.map_or(true, |s| {\n   594→            s.flags\n   595→                .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n   596→        });\n   597→        if !should_traverse {\n   598→            return Self::none();\n   599→        }\n   600→        return Self::NotEvaluated(Box::new(move || {\n   601→            Self::lookup(element, if is_pseudo { known_parent_style } else { None })\n   602→        }));\n   603→    }\n   604→\n   605→    /// Create a new instance, but with optional element.\n   606→    pub fn for_option_element<E>(\n   607→        element: Option<E>,\n   608→        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n   609→        is_pseudo: bool,\n   610→    ) -> Self\n   611→    where\n   612→        E: TElement + 'a,\n   613→    {\n   614→        if let Some(e) = element {\n   615→            Self::for_element(e, known_parent_style, is_pseudo)\n   616→        } else {\n   617→            Self::none()\n   618→        }\n   619→    }\n   620→\n   621→    /// Create a query that evaluates to empty, for cases where container size query is not required.\n   622→    pub fn none() -> Self {\n   623→        ContainerSizeQuery::Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult::default())\n   624→    }\n   625→\n   626→    /// Get the result of the container size query, doing the lookup if called for the first time.\n   627→    pub fn get(&mut self) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n   628→        match self {\n   629→            Self::NotEvaluated(lookup) => {\n   630→                *self = Self::Evaluated((lookup)());\n   631→                match self {\n   632→                    Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n   633→                    _ => unreachable!(\"Just evaluated but not set?\"),\n   634→                }\n   635→            },\n   636→            Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n   637→        }\n   638→    }\n   639→}\n   640→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c9b824cf-efe9-40d1-9d4c-1371550429b3","uuid":"6cde8e5e-2d1b-4737-bc8b-2b8a9614e274","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/tmp/stylo-repo/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs","content":"/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\n//! A [`@container`][container] rule.\n//!\n//! [container]: https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-rule\n\nuse crate::computed_value_flags::ComputedValueFlags;\nuse crate::derives::*;\nuse crate::dom::TElement;\nuse crate::logical_geometry::{LogicalSize, WritingMode};\nuse crate::parser::ParserContext;\nuse crate::properties::ComputedValues;\nuse crate::queries::feature::{AllowsRanges, Evaluator, FeatureFlags, QueryFeatureDescription};\nuse crate::queries::values::Orientation;\nuse crate::queries::{FeatureType, QueryCondition};\nuse crate::shared_lock::{\n    DeepCloneWithLock, Locked, SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard,\n};\nuse crate::stylesheets::{CssRules, CustomMediaEvaluator};\nuse crate::stylist::Stylist;\nuse crate::values::computed::{CSSPixelLength, ContainerType, Context, Ratio};\nuse crate::values::specified::ContainerName;\nuse app_units::Au;\nuse cssparser::{Parser, SourceLocation};\nuse euclid::default::Size2D;\n#[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\nuse malloc_size_of::{MallocSizeOfOps, MallocUnconditionalShallowSizeOf};\nuse selectors::kleene_value::KleeneValue;\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse std::fmt::{self, Write};\nuse style_traits::{CssStringWriter, CssWriter, ParseError, ToCss};\n\n/// A container rule.\n#[derive(Debug, ToShmem)]\npub struct ContainerRule {\n    /// The container query and name.\n    pub condition: Arc<ContainerCondition>,\n    /// The nested rules inside the block.\n    pub rules: Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\n    /// The source position where this rule was found.\n    pub source_location: SourceLocation,\n}\n\nimpl ContainerRule {\n    /// Returns the query condition.\n    pub fn query_condition(&self) -> &QueryCondition {\n        &self.condition.condition\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the query name filter.\n    pub fn container_name(&self) -> &ContainerName {\n        &self.condition.name\n    }\n\n    /// Measure heap usage.\n    #[cfg(feature = \"gecko\")]\n    pub fn size_of(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, ops: &mut MallocSizeOfOps) -> usize {\n        // Measurement of other fields may be added later.\n        self.rules.unconditional_shallow_size_of(ops)\n            + self.rules.read_with(guard).size_of(guard, ops)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl DeepCloneWithLock for ContainerRule {\n    fn deep_clone_with_lock(&self, lock: &SharedRwLock, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) -> Self {\n        let rules = self.rules.read_with(guard);\n        Self {\n            condition: self.condition.clone(),\n            rules: Arc::new(lock.wrap(rules.deep_clone_with_lock(lock, guard))),\n            source_location: self.source_location.clone(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ToCssWithGuard for ContainerRule {\n    fn to_css(&self, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard, dest: &mut CssStringWriter) -> fmt::Result {\n        dest.write_str(\"@container \")?;\n        {\n            let mut writer = CssWriter::new(dest);\n            if !self.condition.name.is_none() {\n                self.condition.name.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n                writer.write_char(' ')?;\n            }\n            self.condition.condition.to_css(&mut writer)?;\n        }\n        self.rules.read_with(guard).to_css_block(guard, dest)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A container condition and filter, combined.\n#[derive(Debug, ToShmem, ToCss)]\npub struct ContainerCondition {\n    #[css(skip_if = \"ContainerName::is_none\")]\n    name: ContainerName,\n    condition: QueryCondition,\n    #[css(skip)]\n    flags: FeatureFlags,\n}\n\n/// The result of a successful container query lookup.\npub struct ContainerLookupResult<E> {\n    /// The relevant container.\n    pub element: E,\n    /// The sizing / writing-mode information of the container.\n    pub info: ContainerInfo,\n    /// The style of the element.\n    pub style: Arc<ComputedValues>,\n}\n\nfn container_type_axes(ty_: ContainerType, wm: WritingMode) -> FeatureFlags {\n    if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n        FeatureFlags::all_container_axes()\n    } else if ty_.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n        let physical_axis = if wm.is_vertical() {\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS\n        } else {\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS\n        };\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS | physical_axis\n    } else {\n        FeatureFlags::empty()\n    }\n}\n\nenum TraversalResult<T> {\n    InProgress,\n    StopTraversal,\n    Done(T),\n}\n\nfn traverse_container<E, F, R>(\n    mut e: E,\n    originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    evaluator: F,\n) -> Option<(E, R)>\nwhere\n    E: TElement,\n    F: Fn(E, Option<&ComputedValues>) -> TraversalResult<R>,\n{\n    if originating_element_style.is_some() {\n        match evaluator(e, originating_element_style) {\n            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((e, result)),\n        }\n    }\n    while let Some(element) = e.traversal_parent() {\n        match evaluator(element, None) {\n            TraversalResult::InProgress => {},\n            TraversalResult::StopTraversal => return None,\n            TraversalResult::Done(result) => return Some((element, result)),\n        }\n        e = element;\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\nimpl ContainerCondition {\n    /// Parse a container condition.\n    pub fn parse<'a>(\n        context: &ParserContext,\n        input: &mut Parser<'a, '_>,\n    ) -> Result<Self, ParseError<'a>> {\n        let name = input\n            .try_parse(|input| ContainerName::parse_for_query(context, input))\n            .ok()\n            .unwrap_or_else(ContainerName::none);\n        let condition = QueryCondition::parse(context, input, FeatureType::Container)?;\n        let flags = condition.cumulative_flags();\n        Ok(Self {\n            name,\n            condition,\n            flags,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn valid_container_info<E>(\n        &self,\n        potential_container: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        let data;\n        let style = match originating_element_style {\n            Some(s) => s,\n            None => {\n                data = match potential_container.borrow_data() {\n                    Some(d) => d,\n                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n                };\n                &**data.styles.primary()\n            },\n        };\n        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n        let box_style = style.get_box();\n\n        // Filter by container-type.\n        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n        let available_axes = container_type_axes(container_type, wm);\n        if !available_axes.contains(self.flags.container_axes()) {\n            return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n        }\n\n        // Filter by container-name.\n        let container_name = box_style.clone_container_name();\n        for filter_name in self.name.0.iter() {\n            if !container_name.0.contains(filter_name) {\n                return TraversalResult::InProgress;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let size = potential_container.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n        let style = style.to_arc();\n        TraversalResult::Done(ContainerLookupResult {\n            element: potential_container,\n            info: ContainerInfo { size, wm },\n            style,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Performs container lookup for a given element.\n    pub fn find_container<E>(\n        &self,\n        e: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> Option<ContainerLookupResult<E>>\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        match traverse_container(\n            e,\n            originating_element_style,\n            |element, originating_element_style| {\n                self.valid_container_info(element, originating_element_style)\n            },\n        ) {\n            Some((_, result)) => Some(result),\n            None => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to match a container query condition for a given element.\n    pub(crate) fn matches<E>(\n        &self,\n        stylist: &Stylist,\n        element: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n        invalidation_flags: &mut ComputedValueFlags,\n    ) -> KleeneValue\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        let result = self.find_container(element, originating_element_style);\n        let (container, info) = match result {\n            Some(r) => (Some(r.element), Some((r.info, r.style))),\n            None => (None, None),\n        };\n        // Set up the lookup for the container in question, as the condition may be using container\n        // query lengths.\n        let size_query_container_lookup = ContainerSizeQuery::for_option_element(\n            container, /* known_parent_style = */ None, /* is_pseudo = */ false,\n        );\n        Context::for_container_query_evaluation(\n            stylist.device(),\n            Some(stylist),\n            info,\n            size_query_container_lookup,\n            |context| {\n                let matches = self\n                    .condition\n                    .matches(context, &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none());\n                if context\n                    .style()\n                    .flags()\n                    .contains(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS)\n                {\n                    // TODO(emilio): Might need something similar to improve\n                    // invalidation of font relative container-query lengths.\n                    invalidation_flags\n                        .insert(ComputedValueFlags::USES_VIEWPORT_UNITS_ON_CONTAINER_QUERIES);\n                }\n                matches\n            },\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n/// Information needed to evaluate an individual container query.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\npub struct ContainerInfo {\n    size: Size2D<Option<Au>>,\n    wm: WritingMode,\n}\n\nimpl ContainerInfo {\n    fn size(&self) -> Option<Size2D<Au>> {\n        Some(Size2D::new(self.size.width?, self.size.height?))\n    }\n}\n\nfn eval_width(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.width?.to_f32_px()))\n}\n\nfn eval_height(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(info.size.height?.to_f32_px()))\n}\n\nfn eval_inline_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n            .inline?\n            .to_f32_px(),\n    ))\n}\n\nfn eval_block_size(context: &Context) -> Option<CSSPixelLength> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(CSSPixelLength::new(\n        LogicalSize::from_physical(info.wm, info.size)\n            .block?\n            .to_f32_px(),\n    ))\n}\n\nfn eval_aspect_ratio(context: &Context) -> Option<Ratio> {\n    let info = context.container_info.as_ref()?;\n    Some(Ratio::new(\n        info.size.width?.0 as f32,\n        info.size.height?.0 as f32,\n    ))\n}\n\nfn eval_orientation(context: &Context, value: Option<Orientation>) -> KleeneValue {\n    let size = match context.container_info.as_ref().and_then(|info| info.size()) {\n        Some(size) => size,\n        None => return KleeneValue::Unknown,\n    };\n    KleeneValue::from(Orientation::eval(size, value))\n}\n\n/// https://drafts.csswg.org/css-contain-3/#container-features\n///\n/// TODO: Support style queries, perhaps.\npub static CONTAINER_FEATURES: [QueryFeatureDescription; 6] = [\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"width\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_width),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_WIDTH_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"height\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_height),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_HEIGHT_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"inline-size\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_inline_size),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"block-size\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalLength(eval_block_size),\n        FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS,\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"aspect-ratio\"),\n        AllowsRanges::Yes,\n        Evaluator::OptionalNumberRatio(eval_aspect_ratio),\n        // XXX from_bits_truncate is const, but the pipe operator isn't, so this\n        // works around it.\n        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n        ),\n    ),\n    feature!(\n        atom!(\"orientation\"),\n        AllowsRanges::No,\n        keyword_evaluator!(eval_orientation, Orientation),\n        FeatureFlags::from_bits_truncate(\n            FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_BLOCK_AXIS.bits()\n                | FeatureFlags::CONTAINER_REQUIRES_INLINE_AXIS.bits()\n        ),\n    ),\n];\n\n/// Result of a container size query, signifying the hypothetical containment boundary in terms of physical axes.\n/// Defined by up to two size containers. Queries on logical axes are resolved with respect to the querying\n/// element's writing mode.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Default)]\npub struct ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n    width: Option<Au>,\n    height: Option<Au>,\n}\n\nimpl ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n    fn get_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> Size2D<Au> {\n        use crate::values::specified::ViewportVariant;\n        context.viewport_size_for_viewport_unit_resolution(ViewportVariant::Small)\n    }\n\n    fn get_logical_viewport_size(context: &Context) -> LogicalSize<Au> {\n        LogicalSize::from_physical(\n            context.builder.writing_mode,\n            Self::get_viewport_size(context),\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inline-size of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_inline_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n            if let Some(w) = self.width {\n                return w;\n            }\n        } else {\n            if let Some(h) = self.height {\n                return h;\n            }\n        }\n        Self::get_logical_viewport_size(context).inline\n    }\n\n    /// Get the block-size of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_block_size(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if context.builder.writing_mode.is_horizontal() {\n            self.get_container_height(context)\n        } else {\n            self.get_container_width(context)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the width of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_width(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if let Some(w) = self.width {\n            return w;\n        }\n        Self::get_viewport_size(context).width\n    }\n\n    /// Get the height of the query container.\n    pub fn get_container_height(&self, context: &Context) -> Au {\n        if let Some(h) = self.height {\n            return h;\n        }\n        Self::get_viewport_size(context).height\n    }\n\n    // Merge the result of a subsequent lookup, preferring the initial result.\n    fn merge(self, new_result: Self) -> Self {\n        let mut result = self;\n        if let Some(width) = new_result.width {\n            result.width.get_or_insert(width);\n        }\n        if let Some(height) = new_result.height {\n            result.height.get_or_insert(height);\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool {\n        self.width.is_some() && self.height.is_some()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Unevaluated lazy container size query.\npub enum ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n    /// Query prior to evaluation.\n    NotEvaluated(Box<dyn Fn() -> ContainerSizeQueryResult + 'a>),\n    /// Cached evaluated result.\n    Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult),\n}\n\nimpl<'a> ContainerSizeQuery<'a> {\n    fn evaluate_potential_size_container<E>(\n        e: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> TraversalResult<ContainerSizeQueryResult>\n    where\n        E: TElement,\n    {\n        let data;\n        let style = match originating_element_style {\n            Some(s) => s,\n            None => {\n                data = match e.borrow_data() {\n                    Some(d) => d,\n                    None => return TraversalResult::InProgress,\n                };\n                &**data.styles.primary()\n            },\n        };\n        if !style\n            .flags\n            .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n        {\n            // We know we won't find a size container.\n            return TraversalResult::StopTraversal;\n        }\n\n        let wm = style.writing_mode;\n        let box_style = style.get_box();\n\n        let container_type = box_style.clone_container_type();\n        let size = e.query_container_size(&box_style.clone_display());\n        if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::SIZE) {\n            TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n                width: size.width,\n                height: size.height,\n            })\n        } else if container_type.intersects(ContainerType::INLINE_SIZE) {\n            if wm.is_horizontal() {\n                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n                    width: size.width,\n                    height: None,\n                })\n            } else {\n                TraversalResult::Done(ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n                    width: None,\n                    height: size.height,\n                })\n            }\n        } else {\n            TraversalResult::InProgress\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Find the query container size for a given element. Meant to be used as a callback for new().\n    fn lookup<E>(\n        element: E,\n        originating_element_style: Option<&ComputedValues>,\n    ) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult\n    where\n        E: TElement + 'a,\n    {\n        match traverse_container(\n            element,\n            originating_element_style,\n            |e, originating_element_style| {\n                Self::evaluate_potential_size_container(e, originating_element_style)\n            },\n        ) {\n            Some((container, result)) => {\n                if result.is_complete() {\n                    result\n                } else {\n                    // Traverse up from the found size container to see if we can get a complete containment.\n                    result.merge(Self::lookup(container, None))\n                }\n            },\n            None => ContainerSizeQueryResult::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new instance of the container size query for given element, with a deferred lookup callback.\n    pub fn for_element<E>(\n        element: E,\n        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n        is_pseudo: bool,\n    ) -> Self\n    where\n        E: TElement + 'a,\n    {\n        let parent;\n        let data;\n        let parent_style = match known_parent_style {\n            Some(s) => Some(s),\n            None => {\n                // No need to bother if we're the top element.\n                parent = match element.traversal_parent() {\n                    Some(parent) => parent,\n                    None => return Self::none(),\n                };\n                data = parent.borrow_data();\n                data.as_ref().map(|data| &**data.styles.primary())\n            },\n        };\n\n        // If there's no style, such as being `display: none` or so, we still want to show a\n        // correct computed value, so give it a try.\n        let should_traverse = parent_style.map_or(true, |s| {\n            s.flags\n                .contains(ComputedValueFlags::SELF_OR_ANCESTOR_HAS_SIZE_CONTAINER_TYPE)\n        });\n        if !should_traverse {\n            return Self::none();\n        }\n        return Self::NotEvaluated(Box::new(move || {\n            Self::lookup(element, if is_pseudo { known_parent_style } else { None })\n        }));\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new instance, but with optional element.\n    pub fn for_option_element<E>(\n        element: Option<E>,\n        known_parent_style: Option<&'a ComputedValues>,\n        is_pseudo: bool,\n    ) -> Self\n    where\n        E: TElement + 'a,\n    {\n        if let Some(e) = element {\n            Self::for_element(e, known_parent_style, is_pseudo)\n        } else {\n            Self::none()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a query that evaluates to empty, for cases where container size query is not required.\n    pub fn none() -> Self {\n        ContainerSizeQuery::Evaluated(ContainerSizeQueryResult::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Get the result of the container size query, doing the lookup if called for the first time.\n    pub fn get(&mut self) -> ContainerSizeQueryResult {\n        match self {\n            Self::NotEvaluated(lookup) => {\n                *self = Self::Evaluated((lookup)());\n                match self {\n                    Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n                    _ => unreachable!(\"Just evaluated but not set?\"),\n                }\n            },\n            Self::Evaluated(info) => *info,\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":640,"startLine":1,"totalLines":640}}}

Without optimizations, WPT might\n# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\nopt-level = 3\n\n# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\nopt-level = 1\n\n[profile.release]\nopt-level = 3\ndebug-assertions = true\n\n# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n[profile.coverage]\ninherits = \"dev\"\nincremental = false\ndebug = false\n\n# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n[profile.medium]\ninherits = \"release\"\nopt-level = 2\nincremental = true\ndebug = \"line-tables-only\"\n\n[profile.production]\ninherits = \"release\"\ndebug-assertions = false\nlto = true\ncodegen-units = 1\nopt-level = \"s\"\n\n[profile.production-stripped]\ninherits = \"production\"\nstrip = true\n\n[profile.profiling]\ninherits = \"release\"\ndebug-assertions = false\ndebug = true\nlto = \"thin\"\ncodegen-units = 1\n\n[patch.crates-io]\n# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n# crate, add that here. Use the form:\n#\n#     <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n#\n# For html5ever:\n#\n# html5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/html5ever\" }\n# markup5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/markup5ever\" }\n# web_atoms = { path = \"../html5ever/web_atoms\" }\n# xml5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/xml5ever\" }\n# tendril = { path = \"../html5ever/tendril\" }\n#\n# Or for \"../webrender/webrender\" }\n# webrender_api = { path = \"../webrender/webrender_api\" }\n# wr_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../webrender/wr_malloc_size_of\" }\n#\n# Or for another Git dependency:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/<repository>\"]\n# <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/rust-content-security-policy\"]\n#
